question: What are the Contract Documents Definition & Incorporation Definitions, Terms, & Conditions?
gold_value: The Contract Documents consist of the Agreement between the Owner and Contractor (hereinafter the Agreement), Conditions of the Contract (General, Supplementary and other Conditions), Drawings, Specifications, Addenda issued prior to execution of the Contract, other documents listed in the Agreement, and Modifications issued after execution of the Contract.

A Modification is a written amendment to the Contract signed by both parties, a Change Order, a Construction Change Directive, or a written order for a minor change in the Work issued by the Owner. The Contract Documents do not include bidding requirements, advertisement or invitation to bid, Instructions to Bidders, sample forms, other information furnished by the Owner in anticipation of receiving bids or proposals, the Contractor’s bid or proposal, or portions of Addenda relating to bidding or proposal requirements. The Contract represents the entire and integrated agreement between the parties and supersedes prior negotiations, representations, or agreements, either written or oral. The Contract may be amended or modified only by a Modification. The Contract Documents shall not create a contractual relationship of any kind between the Contractor and the Architect or the Architect’s consultants, between the Owner and a Subcontractor or a Sub-subcontractor, or between any persons or entities other than the Owner and the Contractor.

The Architect and Owner have the right to modify and supplement the list of items on the Punchlist and to modify or establish the reasonable estimated value of completing or correcting such items. The failure to include an item on the Punchlist does not alter the responsibility of the Contractor to complete all Work in accordance with the Contract Documents. Warranties required by the Contract Documents shall commence on the date of Substantial Completion of the Work or designated portion thereof. The Certificate of Substantial Completion shall be submitted to the Owner and Contractor for their written acceptance of responsibilities assigned to them in the Certificate. question: What are the Payment Terms & Conditions for Retainage Reduction?
gold_value: 12.1.11 The Agent and the Contractor shall agree upon a mutually acceptable procedure for review and approval of payments to Subcontractors, and the percentage of retainage held on Subcontracts, and the Contractor shall execute subcontracts in accordance with those agreements.
predicted_value: For each progress payment made prior to Substantial Completion of the Work, the Owner may withhold ten (10%) percent as retainage from the payment otherwise due. Retainage shall not be held on Fee, General Conditions, and Insurance. Upon fifty (50%) percent of Substantial Completion of the Work, the Contractor may submit for a reduction of the retainage, so that the remaining retainage shall not be less than 5% of the Contract Sum. The retained amount shall be reduced to five (5%) percent on all future applications for payment. At the Owner’s reasonable discretion, retainage may not be released for line items where the Owner reasonably believes it is necessary to protect its interests and ensure any incomplete Work is properly completed. Upon achieving Substantial Completion of the entire Work, the Owner shall continue to hold until final completion of the entire Work (including but not limited to punchlist Work) TWO HUNDRED PERCENT (200%) of the Owner’s reasonable estimate of the value of the punchlist Work, any Claims the Owner may have, and all other amounts that the Owner is entitled to withhold. Retainage with respect to punchlist Work shall be paid upon completion of all such punchlist items to the reasonable satisfaction of the Owner.
explanation: The predicted answer provides details on retainage but does not address the mutually acceptable procedure for review and approval of payments to Subcontractors, which is a key part of the gold answer.
raw_answer: For each progress payment made prior to Substantial Completion of the Work, the Owner may withhold ten (10%) percent as retainage from the payment otherwise due. Retainage shall not be held on Fee, General Conditions, and Insurance. Upon fifty (50%) percent of Substantial Completion of the Work, the Contractor may submit for a reduction of the retainage, so that the remaining retainage shall not be less than 5% of the Contract Sum. The retained amount shall be reduced to five (5%) percent on all future applications for payment. At the Owner’s reasonable discretion, retainage may not be released for line items where the Owner reasonably believes it is necessary to protect its interests and ensure any incomplete Work is properly completed. Upon achieving Substantial Completion of the entire Work, the Owner shall continue to hold until final completion of the entire Work (including but not limited to punchlist Work) TWO HUNDRED PERCENT (200%) of the Owner’s reasonable estimate of the value of the punchlist Work, any Claims the Owner may have, and all other amounts that the Owner is entitled to withhold. Retainage with respect to punchlist Work shall be paid upon completion of all such punchlist items to the reasonable satisfaction of the Owner.

question: What are the Warranty Terms & Conditions?
predicted_value: The Contractor warrants to the Owner and Architect that materials and equipment furnished under the Contract will be of good quality and new unless otherwise required or permitted by the Contract Documents, that the Work will be free from defects not inherent in the quality required or permitted and that the Work will conform to the requirements of the Contract Documents. Work not conforming to these requirements, including substitutions not properly approved and authorized, may be considered defective. The Contractor’s warranty excludes remedy for damage or defect caused by abuse, modification not executed by the Contractor, improper or insufficient maintenance, improper operation, or normal wear and tear and normal usage. If required by the Architect or Owner, the Contractor shall furnish satisfactory evidence as to the kind and quality of materials and equipment. The Contractor further agrees that each Subcontract shall contain a warranty of the work performed thereunder in the same form as the above-stated warranty of Contractor. Included in said warranty shall be the statement that it shall be enforceable directly by the Owner, if the Owner so elects. The warranty of any Subcontractor shall not relieve the Contractor of its warranty as set forth above and the Owner may look to the Contractor, directly, and in the first instance to correct any defects in the Work. The warranties provided in this Section 3.5 shall be in addition to and not in limitation of any other warranties, guarantees or remedies allowed by the Contract Documents or otherwise prescribed by law. The Contractor shall procure and deliver to the Owner, no later than the date claimed by the Contractor as the date of Substantial Completion, all warranties required by the Contract Documents. All warranties under this Section 3.5 shall commence as of the date of Substantial Completion of the Work. In no event shall the commencement of the use of building systems in connection with system testing and trial operations prior to the Substantial Completion of the entire Project be deemed to commence the term of any warranty, it being understood and agreed that any such warranty shall not commence until the Substantial Completion of the entire Project and that Contractor shall obtain, at no additional cost to Owner, any extended warranties required in connection therewith. The Contractor agrees to assign to the Owner, at the time of Substantial Completion of the Work, all manufacturer's warranties required by the Contract Documents relating to materials and labor used in the Work and further agrees to perform the Work in such manner so as to preserve all such manufacturer's warranties. In addition to any warranties described above or contained in the description of the Work attached hereto, Contractor agrees to guarantee all work and to replace without cost to Owner any and all defects due to imperfect workmanship or defects in materials.
explanation: The predicted answer provides a detailed description of warranty terms and conditions, but it does not specifically mention the duration of the warranty, which is '1 year from Substantial Completion' as stated in the gold answer.
raw_answer: The Contractor warrants to the Owner and Architect that materials and equipment furnished under the Contract will be of good quality and new unless otherwise required or permitted by the Contract Documents, that the Work will be free from defects not inherent in the quality required or permitted and that the Work will conform to the requirements of the Contract Documents.

question: What are the As-Built / Record Documents Requirements?
predicted_value: The As-Built / Record Documents requirements include the following: All CAD based work must be provided on the latest release of Autocad or compatible DXF or DWG drawing format. If non-CAD based drawings are allowed, they must be prepared by skilled draftsmen and all site-kept as-built markups must be transferred to a clean set of drawings. Particular attention should be given to concealed work. Required items include CAD based Architectural Drawings (2 hard copies and 1 electronic file), CAD based Mechanical, Electrical, Sprinkler drawings (2 hard copies and 1 electronic file), 2 hard copies of BAS Drawings with all control points and program sequence of operations, and As-Builts not CAD based (2 hard copies and one reproducible copy). All documentation must be submitted in a professional manner by qualified project engineering staff, and final project retainage will not be released until all required documentation is submitted, reviewed, and approved. The Owner reserves the right to have closeout documentation professionally prepared if not received complete within one month of substantial completion, with the cost deducted from the contractor’s final payment.
explanation: The predicted answer provides a detailed list of requirements for As-Built / Record Documents, which includes the need for hard copies at final completion. However, it goes beyond the gold answer by specifying additional details and formats, which are not mentioned in the gold answer.
raw_answer: The As-Built / Record Documents requirements include CAD based work in Autocad or compatible formats, non-CAD based drawings by skilled draftsmen, attention to concealed work, and specific required items such as CAD based Architectural and Mechanical, Electrical, Sprinkler drawings, BAS Drawings, and non-CAD based As-Builts. All documentation must be submitted professionally and final retainage is contingent on approval.

question: What are the Final Payment Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: 9.10.1 Upon receipt of the Contractor’s or the Owner’s written notice that the Work is ready for final inspection and acceptance and upon receipt of a final Application for Payment, the Architect and Owner will promptly make such inspection. When the Owner finds the Work acceptable under the Contract Documents and the Contract fully performed, the Owner will promptly approve Contractor’s final Application for Payments
predicted_value: Final payment terms and conditions include the following: (1) submission of a certificate evidencing that required insurance is in force and will not be canceled without prior notice to the Owner, (2) consent of surety to final payment, (3) documentation of special warranties, and other warranties required by the Contract Documents, (4) other data establishing payment or satisfaction of obligations, such as receipts and releases and waivers of liens, claims, security interests, or encumbrances, (5) submission of a final accounting for the Cost of the Work and a final Application for Payment, (6) issuance of a final Certificate for Payment by the Architect, (7) provision of required close-out documents, including warranties, as-built drawings, manuals, and training for the operation and maintenance of equipment, (8) execution of AIA Document G706, (9) submission of lien waivers executed by the Contractor and Subcontractors, (10) delivery of all spare parts required by the Contract Documents, and (11) final releases from the Contractor and Subcontractors certifying that all payrolls, bills for materials and equipment, and other indebtedness connected with the Work have been paid or will be paid from proceeds of final payment. Final payment shall be made no later than 45 days after satisfaction of these requirements. Acceptance of final payment by the Contractor, Subcontractor, or supplier constitutes a waiver of claims except those previously made in writing and identified as unsettled at the time of final Application for Payment.
explanation: The predicted answer includes some relevant details about final payment terms and conditions but also introduces additional information not present in the gold answer. The core aspects of final inspection, acceptance, and approval are covered, but the prediction is more detailed and includes extra conditions.
raw_answer: Final payment terms and conditions include submission of required insurance certificates, consent of surety, documentation of warranties, data establishing payment or satisfaction of obligations, final accounting and Application for Payment, issuance of final Certificate for Payment, provision of close-out documents, execution of AIA Document G706, submission of lien waivers, delivery of spare parts, and final releases from the Contractor and Subcontractors. Final payment shall be made no later than 45 days after satisfaction of these requirements.
